Directive was sent.
However, the physical count is not yet completed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Treasurer to establish the respective shares of the Barangays out of the RPT collections by the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO);
b. Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to transfer to the respective Barangays their RPT shares; and
c. Municipal Accountant to exert effort in retrieving data and information, conduct reconciliation of the Due to LGUs account with available records and make the necessary adjusting entries for deficiencies noted.
observations.reason: Prior years RPT are transferred immediately. Hence, cannot be immediately transferred. The retrieval, request and reconciliation of the Due to LGUs account from the preceding years are on-going.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that all projects charged against the 20% Development Fund are well planned and implementation-ready to fully attain the desired socio-economic targets and outcomes of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Further verification of CY 2026 AIP is needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue addendum to the MOA regarding the provision of schedule and terms of payment and corresponding surcharges for delayed payment by the Lambunao Water District.
observations.reason: The agency is still waiting for the cessation/termination of Joint Venture to which the LWD is involved.

observations.recommendation:
1.17 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct:
a) The inventory Committee to complete the physical count of Property,
Plant and Equipment and ensure that the specific descriptions of the
PPE are properly indicated in the RPCPPE;
b) The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the General Services
Office, to investigate the noted discrepancies, reconcile the amounts
recorded in the books with the RPCPPE, and effect the necessary
adjustments;
c) The GSO, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to exert
effort in locating the documents needed in providing the details of each
PPE account and to provide the Item Number of each PPE in the
RPCPPE;
d) The GSO and Accounting Office to regularly update the Property
Cards (PC) and PPE Ledger Cards for all PPE accounts; and
e) The Municipal Accountant to reclassify the erroneous entries made
from the CIP-Infrastructure Assets and Building account, and transfer
the tangible items below the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 to
the appropriate semi-expendable asset account.
1.18 We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to implement the
one-time cleansing of PPE accounts as provided under COA Circular No.
2020-006 to finally establish the PPE balances that are verifiable as to
existence, condition and accountability.

2.17 We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to verify and adjust
the discrepancies noted and reconcile the cashbooks and accounting
records regularly so that cash account balances are accurately
presented in the financial statements;
b) Municipal Accountant to prepare the BRS within ten (10) days after
receipt of the bank statements and submit the same monthly to the
Auditor, according to Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No.
96-011;
c) Municipal Accountant to record in the books the outstanding balance
amounting to ₱19,458.25 of CIB-LCCA Account No. 3942-1030-92;
d) Municipal Accountant to record the adjustments for all reconciling
items identified in the BRS; and
e) Municipal Treasurer to submit the cashbooks for the seven bank
accounts identified.

3.18 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a) Inventory Committee to regularly conduct the physical count of
inventories as well as the semi-expendable properties, prepare the
corresponding reports and submit them to the Auditor within the
reglementary period;
b) Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer to prepare the
appropriate ledger cards and stock/property cards, respectively, and
reconcile their records;
c) Concerned Heads of Offices, responsible for the distribution of the
goods and items under the Inventory Held for Distribution accounts, to
submit proof of distribution to the Accounting Office; and
d) Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to
exclude undelivered items, and to, henceforth, record only duly
delivered and received items under the Inventory and Accounts Payable
accounts.

4.19 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Make adjusting entries to record the special purpose funds under the
Trust Liabilities accounts and maintain a subsidiary ledger for each
fund;
b) Review the propriety of classification of the BAWASA Fund and
Registration Fees as trust receipts and submit supporting documents,
otherwise, transfer the same to the General Fund and make the
necessary adjusting entries; and
c) Facilitate the refund the unutilized balances of fund transfers for
completed projects and those that can no longer be implemented to the
source agencies.
4.20 We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to enter into an
agreement with the concerned barangays to amend the existing MOAs to
include a provision regarding the shares of the distribution of the unutilized
funds between the parties.

5.15 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) Identify the cash prizes with or without specific utilization guidelines or
conditions imposed by the awarding body, and record them
accordingly, as either Miscellaneous Income or Trust Liabilities with
the necessary Prior Years’ Adjustments;
b) Make appropriate adjusting entries to transfer special purpose funds to
the Trust Liabilities accounts and maintain a subsidiary ledger for each
fund;
c) Determine the specific structures from the property records that were
the subject of the replacement cost and make adjusting entries to drop
the records from the book; and
d) Transfer the unutilized replacement cost received from the DPWH to
the General Fund and make the necessary adjusting entries.

6.32 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Planning and Development Office and Municipal Engineering Office, to
ensure the optimal utilization of the 20% Development Fund by fully
implementing the programs, projects, and activities included in the AIP to
help achieve the desirable socio-economic targets and outcomes of the
Municipality for the benefit of its constituents.
6.33 We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a) Direct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to
indicate in the AIP the specific PPAs with allocations chargeable against
the 20% DF;
b) Direct the Municipal Accountant to:
b.1) Make the necessary adjusting entries to correctly record the
disbursement for the purchase of CCTV cameras for the public
market, and ensure that future disbursements are recorded under
the appropriate account;
b.2) Submit the six missing disbursement vouchers totaling ₱5.552
million;
b.3) Ensure that all disbursements of government funds are properly
supported with documents;
b.4) Provide justification or explanation for the imposition of
liquidated damages for 30 calendar days instead of the actual
delay period.
c) Require the Municipal Engineer to submit the recommendation for the
suspension of work and resumption of work, if any, to the Municipal
Mayor for approval.
d) In coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal
Treasurer, Municipal Accountant and Municipal Development
Council (MDC):
d.1) Review the unimplemented PPAs under the current and
continuing appropriations to assess their feasibility, and if found
impractical, reprogram the funds for implementable PPAs that
would effectively serve the purpose of the 20% DF; and
d.2) Ensure that the development projects funded under the 20% DF
are well-planned and ready for both procurement and
implementation.

7.15 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Accountant and HRMO to:
a) Formulate and implement policies and guidelines in the selection,
recruitment, and remuneration of Job Order (JO) and Contract of
Service (COS) personnel;
b) Ensure that the duties, functions, and required duty hours JO personnel
are clearly specified in the JO Contract;
c) Provide justification or basis for the hiring of five Job Order personnel
beyond the six-month limit of JO contract; and
d) Indicate in the Accomplishment Report, the quantified and specific
duties and functions performed by the personnel during the period.

8.9 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal
Budget Officer to make the necessary corrections to reflect the unposted
appropriations covered by Supplemental Budget Nos. 2024-001 and 2024-
002.
8.10 We further recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a) Transfer the unexpended Quick Response Fund and LDRRMF-MOOE
for Calendar Year 2024 amounting to ₱7.093 million to the Special
Trust Fund and record the same under the Trust Liability-DRRM
account in the Trust Fund books;
b) Conduct a thorough analysis of the balances comprising the Special
Trust Fund to determine the cause of the discrepancy between the
Subsidiary Ledgers and the corresponding notes, and make necessary
adjustments; and
c) Identify the unutilized balances that are already beyond the five-year
period and revert them to the unappropriated surplus of the General
Fund.

9.12 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the following:
a) The MDRRMO to ensure that the LDRRMFIP is prepared in
accordance with the relevant guidelines, and that it is duly approved by
the Sangguniang Bayan and the Local Chief Executive upon
recommendation of the LDRRM Council; and
b) The Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to process
obligations and disbursements of the LDRRMF only when supported
with a duly approved LDRRMFIP.

10.16 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a) Direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the details or breakdown
of the expenses charged against the GAD Fund;
b) Strengthen the GAD Focal Point System and capacitate its members
by sending them or providing them the necessary training relative to
Gender and Development (GAD);
c) Direct the GFPS to develop and maintain LGU GAD Database as
source of information for gender-responsive planning, programming
and policy formulation; and
d) Ensure the full implementation of GAD PPAs identified and ensure
that the relevant GAD PPAs will address/respond to the gender-
related issues identified in the Municipality.

11.15 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Municipal Treasurer to rigorously enforce the collection of delinquent
Real Property Taxes through an intensified and aggressive tax campaign
and strict adherence to the remedies outlined in Sections 256 to 260 of
Republic Act No. 7160.
11.16 We also recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Municipal Assessor, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer, to
expedite the updating of real property records through a comprehensive
assessment of all properties within the municipality. This assessment
should specifically identify:
a) Properties that have been subdivided but remain under the original
mother title;
b) Properties with issued Certificates of Land Ownership Award
(CLOAs) from the Department of Agrarian Reform (DAR); and
c) Properties donated to public schools and those affected by road right-
of-way issues.

12.13 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
following:
a) OIC-MEEDO to revise the Contract of Lease to incorporate the
provisions for penalties as stipulated in Sections 9 and 10 of the
Market Code; and
b) OIC-MEEDO and Municipal Treasurer to strictly enforce the
collection of the delinquent market rental fees by implementing the
appropriate sanctions provided in the revised Contract of Lease.

13.30 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
following:
a) OIC-MEEDO other concerned personnel and officials to:
a.1) Adhere to the planning and implementing procedures outlined
in Item 4 of the Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of the
Local Economic Enterprise for proposed/new LEEs,
particularly on the preparation of a Feasibility Study and 5-year
Business Plan, and subsequent enactment of ordinance for the
creation of the proposed/new LEE; and
a.2) Comply with the implementation procedures provided in Item 5
of the Manual on the Setting Up and Operation of the Local
Economic Enterprise for existing LEEs, specifically conducting
a performance assessment based on the individual Financial
Self-Sufficiency Evaluation conducted at the end of every 5-year
period and preparing a 5-year Business Plan for the continuance
of its operation.
b) Municipal Treasurer to:
b.1) Identify and properly classify the revenues generated by specific
MEEDO revenue-generating units and ensure that collections
from other sources are recorded under the General Fund of the
Municipality; and
b.2) Implement specific revenue collection procedures, in close
coordination with the OIC-MEEDO, to ensure the achievement
of the established annual revenue targets.
c) Municipal Accountant to:
c.1) Prepare individual financial reports for each MEEDO revenue-
generating unit and submit them to the OIC-MEEDO. These
reports will serve as the basis for the Semi-Annual Financial
Self-Sufficiency Review, which will be used to assess the
performance of existing MEEDO units;
c.2) Identify and document the details of the Transfers/Subsidies to
MEEDO from the General Fund and require repayment or
return of advances once the MEEDO units generate sufficient
income; and
13.31 We also recommended and the Municipal Vice Mayor commits to enact an
ordinance on the creation of a proposed/new LEE based on the Feasibility
Study and Business Plan duly approved by the Municipal Mayor as the
legal basis of its operation.
13.32 Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to ensure
full compliance with the provisions of DBM LBC Nos. 100 and 111 in the
creation and operation of both proposed and existing LEEs to ascertain
the existence, financial sufficiency and viability of these MEEDO revenue-
generating units.

14.15 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed direct the:
a) Municipal Health Officer, in coordination with the Municipal
Engineer and other concerned personnel, to take necessary actions to
utilize the Super Health Center to comply with the condition set forth
in the MOA with the DOH for the release of additional funding;
b) MENRO to secure the MOA with the EMB – Region VI and determine
whether the implementation of the program is still feasible,
otherwise, coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the
disbursement of the refund of the subject fund transfers; and
c) Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer,
to process refund of the unutilized fund received from the Iloilo
Provincial Government amounting to ₱10 million.

15.14 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to properly monitor the taxes withheld through
the preparation of subsidiary ledgers for each kind of withholding
taxes; and
b) Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Treasurer,
to remit intact the full amount of withholding taxes to the BIR within
the prescribed period pursuant to the provisions and BIR Revenue
Regulations Nos. 02-98, as amended.

16.13 We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:
a) Officer or personnel concerned to update the employee records in the
GSIS for the timely remittance of the GSIS personal share,
government share, and loans of the municipal employees;
b) Municipal Accountant to expedite the reconciliation of the
aforementioned accounts in CY 2024 and in the prior years and
prepare a detailed schedule or breakdown of the accounts to arrive at
the correct balance for the period; and
c) Municipal Accountant to see to it that compulsory
contributions/deductions on employees’ compensation are reconciled
and remitted without delay to avoid penalties and unnecessary
inconvenience on the part of employees, such as deprivation of benefits
derived from delayed remittances.

17.11 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:
a) Enforce the settlement and/or liquidation of outstanding cash
advances amounting to ₱19.325 million as of December 31, 2024,
otherwise, suspend the payment of the salaries of accountable officers
until they have fully settled their accounts as provided in Section 5.1
of COA Circular No. 97-002;
b) Require the Municipal Accountant to expedite the reconciliation of the
accounts: Advances for Operating Expenses, Advances to Special
Disbursing Officers and Advances to Officers and Employees and
make the necessary adjusting entries, if warranted, to reflect the
correct balance thereof; and
c) Henceforth, require the accountable officers to liquidate all cash
advances within the required period and enforce the full liquidation of
all cash advances at the end of the year.

18.13 We recommended, and the Municipal Mayor agreed direct the following:
a) The MSWDO to lead the development of internal guidelines for the
implementation of the Sustainable Livelihood Program or other
programs of the MSWDO, and submit the same to the Sanggunian for
approval;
b) The Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements related to
Loans Receivables -Others are supported with Loan Agreements,
Promissory Notes, or other relevant documents; and
c) The MSWDO to establish collection procedures or processes, exert
effort in the repayment of loans and assign personnel to regularly
monitor the collections.
18.14 We further recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to make
the necessary adjusting entries for the erroneous entry made to the
account, Loans Receivable – Others, amounting to ₱587,497.92, and to
reconcile the negative balance amounting to ₱6,650.00 in order reflect the
correct balance in the financial statements of the Municipality.

19.12 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
following:
a) The respective Department Heads to submit their PPMP to the Bids
and Awards Committee (BAC) for consolidation into the APP;
b) The BAC to ensure that APP is duly supported by the required
PPMPs;
c) The Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the BAC, to ensure
that complete supporting documents are attached to the Disbursement
Voucher before processing the payment; and
d) The Municipal Accountant to submit the justification, for processing
of payments despite the absence of BAC resolution recommending the
use of direct contracting in the survey contract and van rental service
contract.

20.21 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the LEDIP
Officer/OIC-MEEDO or the concerned personnel to provide justification
or explanation on the following:
a) The Municipality’s failure to undergo the procurement process for
consultancy services in the formulation of the Strategic Business Plan,
as required under the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184;
b) The signing of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) only after the
completion of the activity;
c) The signing of the Contract of Services by the LEDIP Officer/OIC-
MEEDO instead of the Municipal Mayor and payment made to
individual members of the Project Team, at varying rates;
d) The absence of supporting documents justifying the basis of the rates
for individual Contract of Service personnel in the disbursement
vouchers; and
20.22 Henceforth, we recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to strictly
adhere with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in the
procurement of consultancy services.

21.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
following:
a) The Municipal Engineer to ensure that the Municipality has the
capacity to implement projects by administration, particularly by
ensuring the availability or access to construction equipment;
b) The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to include the projects to be
implemented by administration in the Annual Procurement Plan
(APP); and
c) The Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that the proper
procurement process is followed and all required documentation is
provided.

22.12 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Municipal Treasurer to:
a) Request from the Provincial Treasurer’s Office the breakdown of the
Barangay Basic RPT shares they remitted to the Municipality; and
b) Henceforth, ensure the early release of the Barangay shares of the
Basic Real Property Tax based on the agreement made so as not to
deprive the immediate use of the funds to implement their programs
and projects.

23.10 We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to provide secure
work areas for the Municipal Treasurer and Cashiers, respectively, to
protect government funds and property against risk of loss through theft.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) conduct proper analysis of transactions pertaining to the Due to NGAs and Due to LGUs account and make proper adjustments; b) maintain subsidiary ledgers for each fund received from NGAs and monitor closely the transactions pertaining thereto; c) exert effort in retrieving data and information and coordinate with source agencies concerned to eliminate the prior years’ balances; and d) refund to the source agencies fund transfers that could no longer be implemented.
observations.reason: Some of the prior years’ balances were refunded. The agency is communicating with source agencies. Further verification with the source agencies is on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to verify extensively the reconciling items and draw journal entry vouchers to record the adjustments; b) Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the depository bank for the rectification of bank errors; and c) henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to restore stale and unreleased checks to the Cash in Bank account and payable accounts.
observations.reason: The MTO and Accountant have communicated with the depository bank. Bank reply is still pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to concerned accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances, calling their attention that failure to do so within the specified time frame is subject to administrative accountability and sanctions in line with CSC MC No. 23, series of 2019; b) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to periodically send demand letters to the concerned officials, outlining the time frame or deadline for the officials to liquidate the unliquidated cash advances; otherwise, withhold their salaries in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002; c) Refrain from granting additional cash advances to the concerned Disbursing Officer, Special Disbursing Officers, employees, and elected officials unless their previous cash advances are settled and proper accounting thereof was made; d) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the amount of cash advance is equal to the net amount of the payroll for a pay period; and e) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to ensure liquidation documents are in accordance to the purpose for which the cash advance is granted.
observations.reason: Demand letters were sent to officials and employees but the provisions of the COA Circular were not yet fully imposed for non-compliance. Some cash advances were liquidated; reconciliation is on-going.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a) Direct the Municipal Agriculturist to furnish the distribution lists of inventories released under the Municipal Agriculture Office to the Head of GSO; b) Direct the Head of the GSO to prepare and submit the SSMI together with the RIS of all issued inventories to the Municipal Accountant; c) Henceforth, require the periodic reconciliation by the Municipal Accountant and Head of GSO of their records relative to the procurement and issuances of supplies and materials to avoid misstatements of inventory accounts in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Documents necessary for the adjustment are being retrieved. Physical count and reconciliation are ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
a) Municipal Treasurer to see to it that the date in the Box D or “Received/Payment” portion of the DV is filled-out when the check is released; b) Municipal Accountant to submit the cash advance voucher with complete supporting documents to the COA ATL within seven days after release of check; and c) Discontinue the practice of granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advances are settled and proper accounting thereof are made.
observations.reason: The Agency ceased to grant CF/IF for the year 2025.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to set up separate ledgers in the special trust fund by year for the transferred unutilized balance of DRRMF from the General Fund; b) MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF duly prepared and certified by the Municipal Accountant reflecting the available funds, utilization and balances of LDRRMF in the general fund and the yearly special trust fund; and c) Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the LDRRM Fund in the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and the balances reflected in the Notes to Financial Statements.
observations.reason: The Office of the Municipal Accountant has prepared separate ledger for the transferred unutilized balance for LDR special trust fund. The Office has reconciled the balances of the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and the Notes to Financial Statements.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a) Implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 and record adjustments for valid reconciling items as reflected in the BRS prepared by the Accounting Office. The BRS submitted to the Auditor should be supported by copies of the JEV recording the reconciling items that require correction in the books of accounts, as well as a copy of the letter to the respective banks informing them of errors noted in the BRS. Prior to actual reconciliation, verification of supporting paid checks, debit memos and credit memos should be made upon receipt of the bank statements, to ensure that debits and credit made in the bank statements are properly documented by Bank; and
b) Exert more effort in determining the unreconciled debit/credit memos, and other reconciling items, and properly label the items in the Bank Reconciliation Statements. Cash balances and transactions should be monitored regularly to determine proper use of the accounts.
observations.reason: BRS submitted to the Auditor should be supported by copies of the JEV recording the reconciling items that require correction in the books of accounts, as well as a copy of the letter to the respective banks informing them of errors noted in the BRS. Prior to actual reconciliation, verification of supporting paid checks, debit memos and credit memos should be made upon receipt of the bank statements, to ensure that debits and credit made in the bank statements are properly documented by Bank; and the Agency will communicate with our Depositary bank.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
a) Require from the concerned AOs the immediate liquidation of their outstanding cash advance and refund the unutilized balances; b) Direct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to ensure that the amount of cash advance is equal to the net amount of the payroll for a pay period; c) Instruct the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the liquidation documents are in accordance to the purpose which the cash advance is granted; d) Cease the granting additional cash advances to AOs with unliquidated cash advances; and e) Direct the Municipal Accountant to send out demand letters periodically to concerned officials indicating the period or deadlines within which the concerned official may liquidate the cash advances, otherwise, withholding of the payment of the cash advances, otherwise, their salaries shall be made in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular no. 97-002.
observations.reason: Demand letters were sent to Disbursing Officers. Some balances remain unliquidated/unrefunded; reconciliation and follow-up are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive observe the required procurement process prescribed by the IRR of RA No. 9184. We likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all officials concerned to discontinue the practice of reimbursement basis in procuring goods and services and draw check payments directly in the name of the business establishment in conformity with existing rules and regulations and withheld the appropriate taxes due the government.
observations.reason: The Agency only allows reimbursement on urgent cases. Further verification/post-audit of succeeding transactions is needed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require all municipal officials and employees concerned to settle their audit suspension and disallowances in the total amount of ₱1.187 million (net of disallowances under appeal of ₱5.498 million) pursuant to Section 5.4 of the RRSA.
observations.reason: The disallowance is no longer part of the NSSDC issued. Settlement and appeal processes are ongoing for other outstanding amounts.
Reiterated in this report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the local Chief Executive agreed to require the municipal officials concerned to identify the land owned by the Municipality and undertake immediate action to expedite the titling of the land in the name of the Municipality where government properties are located so as to prevent the occurrence of dispute over the use of the properties in the future.
observations.reason: Some portion of lots were donated. The Municipal Assessor is still checking the existence and ownership of some lots.
Reiterated in this year’s report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Reconcile the balance of the DRRMF fund in the Special Trust Fund and make proper adjusting and correcting entries for erroneous recording and reflect the accurate balances; and

Revisit COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and Observe the proper accounting and recording of DRRM transactions in the General and Special Trust funds.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Set up subsidiary ledgers for Trust Liability-LDRRM per year of transfer from the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund; and

Reconcile the balances of the LDRRM Special Trust Fund per year and revert the unutilized balance in the Special Trust Fund beyond the 5- year period to the General Fund.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive observe the required procurement process prescribed by the IRR of RA No. 9184.


We likewise recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all officials concerned to discontinue the practice of reimbursement basis in procuring goods and services and draw check payments directly in the name of the business establishment in conformity with existing rules and regulations and withheld the appropriate taxes due the government.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
 
Direct the Municipal Accountant to implement the provisions of COA Circular No. 96-011 on the reconciliation of the bank statements with the General Ledgers and record adjustments for valid reconciling items as reflected in the BRS. The BRS submitted to the Auditor should be supported by copies of the JEV recording reconciling items that require correction in the books of accounts.  Prior to actual reconciliation, verification of supporting paid checks, debit memos and credit memos should be made upon receipt of the bank statements,  to ensure that debits and credits made in the bank statements are properly documented by the bank;
 
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to exert more effort in determining other reconciling items and properly label the items in the bank reconciliation statements. Cash balances and transactions should be regularly monitored to determine the accuracy of the cash accounts; and
 
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to submit a copy of the letter to the respective banks informing them of errors noted in the bank statements.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this year’s report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the General Services Officer to record the receipt of equipment in the property cards upon acceptance of delivered equipment and forward the AIR and supporting documents to the Accounting unit for recording of deliveries in the Journal of Purchases Received and in the PPE Ledger Cards pursuant to Section 6.3 COA Circular No. 2020-006; and

Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Acting Property Officer that henceforth, PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards shall be maintained for reconciliation, control and monitoring of PPEs.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Provincial Treasurer to establish the respective shares of the Barangays out of the RPT collections by the Provincial Treasurer’s Office (PTO);
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to transfer to the respective Barangays their RPT shares; and
Municipal Accountant to exert effort in retrieving data and information, conduct reconciliation of the Due to LGUs account with available records and make the necessary adjusting entries for deficiencies noted

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant to verify extensively the reconciling items and draw journal entry vouchers to record the adjustments;
Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the depository bank for the rectification of bank errors; and
Henceforth, require the Municipal Accountant to restore stale and unreleased checks to the Cash in Bank account and payable accounts.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to issue demand letters to concerned accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances, calling their attention that failure to do so within the specified time frame is subject to administrative accountability and sanctions in line with CSC MC No. 23, series of 2019;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to periodically send demand letters to the concerned officials, outlining the time frame or deadline for the officials to liquidate the cash advances; otherwise, withhold their salaries in compliance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
Refrain from granting additional cash advances to the concerned Disbursing Officer, Special Disbursing Officers, employees, and elected officials unless their previous cash advances are settled and proper accounting thereof was made.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Record the purchase of rescue boat in the Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment Account; and
Revisit COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and observe the proper accounting and recording of DRRM transactions in the General and Special Trust funds.

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that all projects charged against the 20% Development Fund are well planned and implementation-ready to fully attain the desired socio-economic targets and outcomes of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
Municipal Treasurer to see to it that the date in the Box D or the “Received Payment” portion of the DV is filled-out when the check is released;
Municipal Accountant to submit the cash advance voucher with complete supporting documents to the COA ATL within seven days after release of check; and
Discontinue the practice of granting additional cash advances unless the previous cash advances are settled and proper accounting thereof are made.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the:
Municipal Accountant to set up separate ledgers in the special trust fund by year for the transferred unutilized balance of DRRMF from the General Fund;
MDRRMO to submit to the Audit Team a monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF duly prepared and certified by the Municipal Accountant reflecting the available funds, utilization and balances of LDRRMF in the general fund and the yearly special trust fund; and
Municipal Accountant to reconcile the balances of the LDRRM Fund in the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and the balances reflected in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Treasurer to send out statements of account monthly to market stallholders reminding them of their monthly rentals to improve collection efficiency.

We further recommend and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to send demand letters to tenants/lessees of market stalls with unpaid accounts amounting to ₱413,102.00 and to impose necessary sanctions to those who do not comply/adhere to the provisions  stipulated in the Revenue Code of the Municipality.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to instruct the MSWDO to prepare and formulate specific guidelines in the granting of medical, hospitalization, burial, and other assistance for uniformity and fairness, as well as, to avoid possible misuse of government funds.
12.10 We further recommended and the Sangguniang Bayan agreed to review the policies/guidelines formulated in the granting of financial assistance to individuals and families in crisis.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to: 

a. Required the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the amount of RPT and SET receivables per books based on the certified list from the Treasurer’s office and make the necessary journal entries to adjust the receivable accounts; and

b. Require the Municipal Treasurer to regularly furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly certified list showing the name of the taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year that shall be the basis of the latter to set-up the correct amount of RPT/SET Receivables at the beginning of the accounting period to conform with Sections 19 and 20, Volume I of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

Make journal entries reclassifying Bail Bonds Payable account to Guaranty/ Security Deposits Payable account;

Analyze carefully the transaction and ensure that appropriate account titles are used in recording the transaction in the books; and

Identify the payees/contractors whom the amount of ₱212,038.99 pertained and cause the refund of forfeiture of the amount, if warranted.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Intensify efforts to enforce collection remedies provided by Republic Act. No. 7160, particularly the sale of delinquent properties through public auction in order to demonstrate the Municipality’s will power and capability to enforce the laws on real property taxation and deter tax evasions; and 

Conduct more extensive tax campaigns so that taxpayers are apprised of the advantages/services the Municipality could provide if taxes are paid.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed that in the preparation of the updated Annual Investment Program and Annual Procurement Plan, the Municipality comply with the necessary planning and reporting requirements and in the prescribed format in accordance with DBM Local Budget Circular No. 124, DILG DBM Circular No. 02 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 as the basis for procurement activities.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to direct the SWM Board and MENRO to:

Monitor the implementation and effectiveness of the ESWMP in order that measures to correct the deficiencies noted will be timely instituted; and 

Revisit Republic Act 9003 and comply with the provisions of Sections 12 of the aforementioned law.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Bids and Awards Committee and other concerned officials to:

Submit the supplier’s FDA Special Certification; otherwise, submit a justification as to why the award was given to the Supplier despite the non-submission of the said documentary requirements; and 

Provide the details or necessary information in the supporting documents inclusive of Purchase Requests and Purchase Orders as provided in Section 18 of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of the RA No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 96-010, respectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Instruct the Head of the BAC Secretariat and Municipal Accountant to attach the documents required for procurement through public bidding pursuant to Section 9.1 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure reliability and legality of transactions; and

Direct the Bids and Awards Committee to thoroughly conduct the Post Qualification Evaluation and ensure that bidders comply with the requirements and procedures otherwise, they shall be declared ineligible for competitive bidding or disqualified from obtaining an award or contract.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive Direct the concerned officials and employees to submit the Requisition and Issue Slips, Relief Distribution Sheets and Report of Supplies and Materials Issued indicating actual issuances and distribution of the welfare goods.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the MSWDO to submit the following:
 
Specific documentary requirements submitted by SAP beneficiaries such as valid IDs for each categories and other applicable requirements, as provided in Section 8.1 of DSWD, DOLE, DTI, DA, DOF, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 series of 2020, and DSWD Memorandum Circular No. 9 series of 2020

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:

Direct the Disbursing Officer and Special Disbursing Officers to submit the liquidation reports together with the required supporting documents such as Official Receipts, Purchase Requests, Acceptance and Inspection Report and Summary of Purchases if goods were procured from various stores amounting to ₱150,000.00 and ₱0.900 million, respectively. 

Direct the Disbursing Officer and Special Disbursing Officers to submit the liquidation reports together with supporting documents amounting to ₱150,000.00 and ₱0.900 million, respectively.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to submit the lacking supporting documents for payment of cremation and hospital/medical assistance amounting to ₱275,469.78 pursuant to DBM LBC Circular No. 125, Presidential Decree No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the BAC and the BAC secretariat to prepare the APP and submit to the GPPB in order to comply with the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 and its amendments so that all procurement will be efficiently, effectively and economically managed and ensure the transparency in the Municipality’s transactions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant closely monitor the submission of the DVs and supporting documents so as to facilitate submission to the Auditor within the first ten (10) days of the ensuing month pursuant to concerned regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant see to it that disbursement vouchers were properly signed and authorized by approving officials duly supported with complete documentation before transmittal to the Municipal Treasurer for preparation of checks.

We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure the completeness of signature of approving officials in the disbursement vouchers, to avoid suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer reconcile the accounting and cashbook balances every end of the month and that of prior years’ adjustment of ₱257,417.13.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive regulate the utilization of financial assistance and ensure that expenses intended for socio-cultural activities be expended pursuant to the provisions of the Section 4(2) of PD 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012.

We also recommended that the Sangguniang Bayan draft guidelines to be followed in the payment of financial assistance to ensure proper identification of beneficiaries and appropriate procedure in the grant and disbursement of funds.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant enroll in the Internet Banking Facility for Institutional Clients of the Land Bank of the Philippines (weAccess) to secure updated bank statements.
